However, I don't have access to the actual symbol files or a symbol server. Let me check what tools are available. I have `git`, `curl`, and `gh`. 

For .NET runtime symbols, Microsoft publishes them on their symbol server. The Build IDs in the tombstone can be used to look up symbols. The Microsoft symbol server is at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/`.


For ELF files on Linux/Android, I need to construct the symbol server path using the filename and Build ID in the format `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/<filename>/<buildid>/<filename>`. I have the Build IDs for each library, so I can attempt to fetch the symbol files from Microsoft's server.

Now I'm thinking about the best approach to resolve these addresses—I should check what symbolication tools are available on the system, like `llvm-symbolizer` or `addr2line`, before trying to download the symbols.
